title: Aureline command-diagnostic projection, disabled-reason, and protected-entry target/origin badge contract
description: Boundary schema for the command_diagnostic_row_record, the protected_entry_badge_record, and the remediation_projection_record frozen in docs/ux/command_diagnostics_contract.md. A command_diagnostic_row_record is the governed projection every command palette, application / context menu, toolbar button, keybinding editor, keybinding help, CLI help, AI-tool surface, automation-recipe response, docs reference page, About / service-health panel, command-diagnostics panel, and support-export surface reads before rendering a typed disabled / hidden reason, a typed remediation, a keybinding-help pivot, a docs-help pivot, a route / exposure context, or a target / origin badge. A protected_entry_badge_record is the typed envelope a terminal / tasks / debug / remote-attach / browser-handoff / provider-bearing / managed-workspace-control / credential-broker-step-up / policy-authoring entry carries so every rendering surface reads one target-kind, one target-trust, one origin-actor, one origin-authority, one origin-client-scope, one target-route, one required-trust-state-floor, one required-grant-scope-floor, and one export-safe-wording-class. A remediation_projection_record is the typed repair projection a disabled row carries so every surface renders the same grant-scope requirement, actionability class, and optional next-step deep-link target kind. Raw hostnames, raw URLs, raw deep-link strings, raw account handles, raw project identifiers, raw command bodies, raw argument values, raw prompt text, and raw credential material never cross this boundary; this schema carries opaque ids and typed vocabulary only. The eventual command-registry / invocation-session / diagnostics-surface crates' Rust types are the schema of record; this file is the cross-tool boundary every non-owning surface reads. Adding a new rendering-surface class, protected-entry class, target-kind class, target-trust class, target-route class, actionability class, export-safe-wording class, origin-actor class, required-trust-state-floor value, or required-grant-scope-floor value is additive-minor and requires a diagnostic_projection_schema_version bump; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

diagnostic_projection_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this diagnostic-projection export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape. Every record carries exactly one kind.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests / journal entries / support bundles / claim manifests. Raw hostnames, raw URLs, raw deep-link strings, raw account handles, raw command bodies, raw argument values, raw paths, raw prompt text, and raw credential material never appear here.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

authority_class.description: Re-exported from schemas/commands/command_descriptor.schema.json#authority_class and schemas/ux/interaction_safety.schema.json#authority_class without modification. A surface that cannot resolve its authority class MUST deny with authority_class_unresolved rather than defaulting to user_initiated_local.

capability_scope_class.description: Re-exported from schemas/commands/command_descriptor.schema.json#capability_scope_class without modification. Silent promotion of the scope class at projection time is forbidden; a diagnostic row MUST quote the descriptor's declared scope class verbatim.

rendering_surface_class.description: Frozen rendering-surface vocabulary. Names the surface the diagnostic row is being projected for. A surface that cannot resolve its rendering class MUST deny rather than default to command_palette.

disabled_reason_code.description: Re-exported from schemas/commands/command_descriptor.schema.json#disabled_reason_code without modification. Every disabled / hidden row MUST carry exactly one code; silent disable with no typed reason is non-conforming.

client_scope.description: Re-exported from schemas/governance/capability_lifecycle.schema.json without modification (ADR-0011). A row whose client_scope excludes the rendering surface MUST carry disabled_reason_code = client_scope_excludes_surface.

display_label.description: Short human-legible label the surface may render next to the disabled / denied chip.

provider_actor_class.description: Re-exported from ADR-0010 provider_actor_class (schemas/integration/browser_handoff_packet.schema.json and the approval-ticket contract) without modification. Names who the origin acts as on the provider side; raw credential material never appears here.

deep_link_target_kind.description: Re-exported from schemas/commands/shareability_metadata.schema.json#deep_link_target_kind without modification. A non-null follow-up MUST still re-run the trust / policy / permission / preview / approval / credential-broker / managed-channel / execution-context / client-scope / publisher / kill-switch / freshness gates before any consequence.

protected_entry_class.description: Frozen protected-entry-class vocabulary. Names which category of protected entry the badge attaches to. Adding a new class is additive-minor and bumps diagnostic_projection_schema_version.

target_kind_class.description: Frozen target-kind vocabulary. Names what the protected entry is targeting without exposing raw hostnames, raw URLs, raw account handles, or raw project identifiers. Adding a new kind is additive-minor.

target_trust_class.description: Frozen target-trust vocabulary. Names the trust posture of the target surface. unknown is admissible only when the target has not yet been resolved; the badge MUST still carry required_trust_state_floor.

target_route_class.description: Frozen target-route vocabulary. Names how the user / AI / extension / recipe / deep-link arrived at the protected entry. restored_session_reconnect_route and external_deep_link_route reopen origin and target disclosure regardless of prior grants.

actionability_class.description: Frozen actionability vocabulary. A surface that exposes a user-facing 'Fix this' button MUST verify the row's actionability_class is actionable_by_user or actionable_by_workspace_admin (when the viewer holds that role); otherwise the surface renders the remediation as read-only guidance.

export_safe_wording_class.description: Frozen export-safe-wording vocabulary. Rows / badges declare their wording class so a support bundle, a docs screenshot, or a redaction-safe recording uses the correct posture. A surface rendering a row in a public docs screenshot MUST narrow to safe_for_public_docs; a managed_only_restricted row rendered in public docs is non-conforming.

trust_state.description: ADR-0001 workspace-trust state at the moment the record was minted.

execution_context_id.description: ADR-0009 execution-context id when the record was minted inside a live execution. Null when minted outside an execution context.

active_policy_bundle_ref.description: Opaque pin to the active ADR-0008 policy bundle the evaluation resolved under. Null when no policy bundle is active; non-null for every managed / policy-forced row.

policy_context.description: Policy context the record was minted under. Re-validated on every render.

contextual_filter_class_ref.description: Opaque pin to the contextual-filter class re-exported from the descriptor's ui_slot_hint (e.g. filter:explorer:folder_row, filter:editor:rust_buffer, filter:review:pr_row). Null when the slot is unconditional.

client_scope.description: Client scope the rendering surface belongs to. A row whose client_scope excludes the rendering surface MUST carry disabled_reason_code = client_scope_excludes_surface.

route_and_exposure_context.description: Route / exposure context block. Pins the slot, palette visibility, optional contextual filter, and client scope the row was projected under so support exports and parity audits read the same context.

shortcut_narration_hint_ref.description: Opaque pin to the descriptor's shortcut_narration_hint. Re-exported verbatim; minting a parallel narration is non-conforming.

keybinding_resolver_row_ref.description: Opaque pin to the keybinding-resolver row whose precedence stack won for this command in this context (schemas/commands/keybinding_resolver.schema.json). Null when no shortcut is bound.

chord_class_hint.description: Optional chord-class hint re-exported from the descriptor's shortcut_narration_hint.chord_class_hint. Null when the descriptor did not publish a hint.

keybinding_help_pivot.description: Keybinding-help pivot. Every keybinding-help layer, accessibility layer, and diagnostics panel reads this block to narrate the bound shortcut.

docs_help_anchor_ref.description: Re-exported from the descriptor's docs_help_anchor_ref verbatim; minting a parallel anchor is non-conforming.

docs_help_pivot.description: Docs-help pivot. Every palette tooltip, menu help, keybinding-help expansion, CLI --help output, AI-tool description, diagnostics panel, and docs reference page reads this block.

allOf.description: Reasons whose resolution is gated on policy / lifecycle / publisher / kill-switch MUST NOT be dismissible_by_user.
Reasons whose resolution waits on lifecycle progression MUST declare actionability_class = not_actionable_wait_for_lifecycle.
Reasons whose resolution is administrative MUST declare actionability_class in the admin-actionable set.

remediation_projection_record.description: Remediation projection emitted alongside a disabled / hidden diagnostic row. Every row whose reason is in the remediable set MUST reference exactly one of these records; rows whose reason is not_actionable_* carry no remediation_projection_ref.

command_id_ref.description: Opaque pin to the command descriptor the badge attaches to. Null when the badge attaches to a surface-level entry that is not a descriptor-level command.

command_revision_ref.description: Opaque pin to the descriptor revision the badge was resolved against. Null when command_id_ref is null.

surface_entry_id_ref.description: Opaque pin to the surface-level entry the badge attaches to when it is not a descriptor-level command (a terminal chord entering a paste-review state, a remote-attach rail entry, a browser-handoff provider tile). Null when command_id_ref is non-null.

target_identity_ref.description: Opaque pin to the target identity (local shell handle, remote agent session, debug adapter instance, browser-handoff target, provider account / project, managed workspace tenant, secret-broker scope, policy bundle). Raw hostnames, raw URLs, raw account handles, raw project identifiers, and raw credential material never appear here.

origin_identity_ref.description: Opaque pin to the origin identity the broker holds. Raw credential material never appears here.

required_trust_state_floor.description: Minimum ADR-0001 workspace-trust state under which the badge admits apply. Badges whose floor is not met pair with a row carrying workspace_trust_restricted.

applicable_surface_classes.description: Closed set of rendering surfaces that MUST render the same badge for the same (command_id, command_revision_ref, target_identity_ref, origin_identity_ref) tuple. Silent divergence is non-conforming.

allOf.description: Exactly one of command_id_ref or surface_entry_id_ref is non-null.
Terminal-entry badges MUST target a shell kind.
Remote-attach-entry badges MUST target a remote-attach session.
Debug-entry badges MUST target a debug adapter session.
Tasks-entry badges MUST target a task runner session.
Browser-handoff-entry badges MUST target a browser-handoff external site.
Provider-bearing-action-entry badges MUST target a provider mutation.
Managed-workspace-control-entry badges MUST target the managed-workspace control plane.
Credential-broker-step-up-entry badges MUST target a credential-broker scope.
Policy-authoring-or-waiver-entry badges MUST target a policy bundle.
Managed-control-plane targets MUST declare a managed_only wording class.

protected_entry_badge_record.description: Protected-entry target/origin badge every terminal, tasks, debug, remote-attach, browser-handoff, provider-bearing-action, managed-workspace-control, credential-broker-step-up, or policy-authoring-or-waiver entry carries. Silent target / origin drift across surfaces is detected mechanically by parity audits reading the applicable_surface_classes set.

invocation_session_id_ref.description: Opaque pin to the invocation-session packet the row projects from. Null when the row is produced for a surface that renders disabled / hidden commands without dispatching them (palette listing, keybinding editor, diagnostics panel).

disabled_reason_label_ref.description: Opaque pin to the export-safe short label a surface renders next to the chip. Resolved by the docs / localisation pipeline.

long_explanation_label_ref.description: Opaque pin to the long-form narration a screen reader / accessibility layer reads.

enablement_decision.description: Enablement decision block. disabled_with_reason / hidden_with_reason MUST carry disabled_reason_code, disabled_reason_label_ref, and long_explanation_label_ref.

remediation_projection_ref.description: Opaque pin to the remediation_projection_record attached to this row. Null when the reason is non-remediable (command_retired, command_version_unknown, capability_lifecycle_retired); non-null otherwise when the decision is disabled_with_reason / hidden_with_reason.

protected_entry_badge_ref.description: Opaque pin to the protected_entry_badge_record attached to this row. Non-null when the protected-entry selection rules in docs/ux/command_diagnostics_contract.md apply; null otherwise.

parity_surface_set.description: Closed set of rendering-surface classes that MUST render this same row for the same (command_id, command_revision_ref, policy_epoch, trust_state, issuing_surface, ui_slot_class, execution_context_id) tuple. MUST include the record's own rendering_surface_class.

allOf.description: disabled_with_reason / hidden_with_reason decisions MUST carry a typed disabled_reason_code, an export-safe short label, and a long-form narration.
enabled decisions MUST carry a null disabled_reason_code.
Reasons whose resolution waits on lifecycle progression MUST carry a null remediation_projection_ref.
Credential-bearing / managed / policy-authoring / externally-visible-mutation / irreversible-high-blast capability scopes MUST carry a protected_entry_badge_ref.
Protected preview classes MUST carry a protected_entry_badge_ref.
managed_only_restricted rows MUST NOT render in the docs_reference_page_surface (it is a public surface).
operator_only_restricted and managed_only_restricted rows MUST NOT render in the docs_reference_page_surface.

command_diagnostic_row_record.description: Canonical command-diagnostic row projection every palette, menu, toolbar, keybinding editor, keybinding help, CLI help, AI-tool surface, automation-recipe response surface, docs reference page surface, about / service-health panel, command-diagnostics panel, and support-export surface reads before rendering a disabled / hidden / enabled chip, a typed remediation, a keybinding-help pivot, a docs-help pivot, a route / exposure block, or a protected-entry badge. Adding a new field is additive-minor; repurposing an existing field is breaking and requires a new decision row.
